title: Coptophyllum nicobaricum
intro: Coptophyllum nicobaricum is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly found in the Nicobar Islands of the Indian Ocean. The plant is known for its traditional medicinal uses, particularly in reducing inflammation and improving respiratory health, with reported benefits including anti-asthmatic, anti-allergic, and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of C. nicobaricum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, decoctions, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into local remedies for respiratory issues and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Coptophyllum nicobaricum is a species of plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Coptophyllum and is a member of the order Gentianales. Coptophyllum nicobaricum is native to the Nicobar Islands in the Bay of Bengal. This species of plant is a part of the angiosperm group, which includes flowering plants. The classification of Coptophyllum nicobaricum is further divided into kingdom Plantae.
